message:
fix(btree): repair insert separator-key maintenance and add proptests

The insert path did not update parent separator keys when a newly
inserted key was smaller than the existing separator for a child node.
This violated the B-tree invariant that each index entry's logical_block
equals the first key in the corresponding child subtree.

Fix: after insert_descend returns at both root (insert fn) and internal
node (insert_descend fn) levels, check if extent.logical_block < the
old separator and update accordingly. Each level independently detects
and fixes its own separator, so the update correctly propagates up the
entire path.

Also adds 7 new property-based tests (8 total) covering insert/search
roundtrip, walk completeness, delete range precision, interleaved
insert/delete invariants, search-missing-returns-hole, multi-block
extent search, and insert-then-delete-all-yields-empty. Includes a
targeted regression test for the minimal failing case from proptest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
